title: Wave Fill, Terminal Panels, Ink Bleed, Glitch & Hover-Play
description: Five patterns riding 2026's counter-trend against overly-polished UI: a liquid wave loader, terminal-styled panels, ink-bleed type, layered glitch-on-hover, and lazy hover-to-play media cards.

drops.title: Liquid Wave Fill
drops.description: A pure-CSS circular tank loader whose surface sloshes with a real wavy top edge, not just a rising bar.
drops.concept: Two layers — a background-position-scrolling wave sprite and a clip-path polygon fill — share the same fill keyframes but run on a calculated negative animation-delay so they finish in lockstep with no visible seam. After filling, three staggered wave layers loop forever at odd, mutually-prime durations so the water motion never repeats identically.
drops.source: coiger/fill-water-animation — "Fill wavy water in circle with pure CSS code"

interaction.mouse: Drag the debug-panel sliders to change fill level and wave speed
interaction.touch: Sliders are touch-draggable; debug panel hidden on small screens

drops.title: Terminal-UI Panels
drops.description: A dashboard of panels, badges and buttons styled entirely like a terminal — monospace grid, box-drawn borders, swappable color themes.
drops.concept: Instead of utility classes, every element is styled through semantic attribute-style rules and CSS custom properties (--term-bg, --term-fg, --term-accent) so an entire theme swap is one classList toggle plus four custom-property writes. A repeating-linear-gradient scanline overlay finishes the CRT illusion.
drops.source: webtui/webtui — "Modular CSS Library that brings the beauty of Terminal UIs to the browser"

interaction.mouse: Click a theme button to swap the entire panel's color scheme
interaction.click: Theme buttons and demo controls are all clickable
interaction.touch: Theme buttons are tap targets sized for touch

drops.title: Ink Bleed Text
drops.description: Headlines that look like ink soaking into paper — soft blur snapped back into hard, torn edges.
drops.concept: A CSS blur filter first spreads each glyph's edges into semi-transparent halo pixels. A single SVG feComponentTransfer with a discrete alpha table then thresholds that halo — low-alpha edge pixels snap to fully transparent, everything else snaps to full opacity — producing a bled, print-like edge instead of smooth anti-aliasing.
drops.source: Andy Jakubowski — "Ink bleed effect with SVG filters"

interaction.mouse: Hover a headline to bleed; the debug panel tunes blur radius and threshold steps
interaction.click: Click a headline to lock the bleed on/off
interaction.touch: Tap a headline to toggle the bleed state

drops.title: Glitch-on-Hover
drops.description: Any element — headline, button, or card — clones into layered horizontal bands that jitter and channel-shift independently.
drops.concept: Each target's DOM is cloned into N absolutely-positioned copies, one per horizontal band via clip-path: inset(). Every band runs the same jitter keyframe but at its own randomized duration and negative delay, plus a hue-rotate filter alternating per band for a chromatic-aberration read, so the glitch never plays back identically twice.
drops.source: 7PH/powerglitch — "Tiny JS library to glitch anything on the web"

interaction.mouse: Hover any of the three elements to trigger the glitch
interaction.click: Switch play mode to "On click" in the debug panel for a timed pulse
interaction.touch: Tap an element to trigger a timed glitch pulse

drops.title: Hover-to-Play Media Cards
drops.description: Static thumbnail cards that lazy-load into looping motion once they're near the viewport, and auto-pause off-screen.
drops.concept: An IntersectionObserver defers starting any motion until the card is actually visible, so nothing runs off-screen. Desktop hover starts playback after a short delay (so a quick mouse pass-by doesn't trigger it) and a coarse-pointer tap toggles play/pause on touch devices; scrolling the card out of view force-pauses it. This playground ships zero external assets, so a canvas-generated motion loop stands in for real video per card.
drops.source: Cult UI — Hover Video Player component

interaction.mouse: Hover a card to start its loop; move away to pause
interaction.click: On touch devices, tap to play/pause
interaction.touch: Tap to toggle playback; scrolling off-screen force-pauses
